research.status_summary: Vox Media (parent of Future Perfect) is mid-acquisition: James Murdoch's Lupa Systems agreed in May 2026 to buy Vox.com, New York Magazine, and the Vox Media Podcast Network for ~$300M, with Future Perfect staying under the Vox.com umbrella; remaining brands (Eater, The Verge, SB Nation, etc.) may go to Penske Media — the company is structurally splitting and in transition, not actively hiring at scale.
research.hiring_policy: Future Perfect specifically posted a Deputy Editor role (Feb 2026) and a new class of Fellows (Jul 2026–Jun 2027, deadline May 12) — both NY/DC-based, US-focused; no public EOR or visa sponsorship language found, and all postings specify US office locations, consistent with the existing 'US-only' note.
recent_news: May 20, 2026: James Murdoch's Lupa Systems agreed to acquire Vox.com (which houses Future Perfect), New York Magazine, and the Vox Media Podcast Network for reportedly >$300M — deal expected to close in 4–6 weeks.
January 2026: Vox Media Union reported 8+ layoffs (staff from Pop Sugar, The Verge, Eater) — the union noted layoffs had occurred 'at an average of once per quarter in the last year.'

Future Perfect itself showed active program continuity: recruiting a new 2026–2027 fellow cohort and a Deputy Editor, with Editorial Director Bryan Walsh shifting to a writing-focused role — no cuts reported within the section itself.
research.growth_signal: Future Perfect appears editorially stable and continuing its fellowship pipeline, but sits inside a company being broken up and sold at a discounted ~$300M valuation — down sharply from the implied ~$500M valuation when Penske invested in 2023 — signaling financial contraction at the parent level even as the section itself keeps publishing.
research.fit_for_lior: Future Perfect covers AI, emerging tech, and global risk through an evidence-based lens, which overlaps with Lior's Creative AI thread, but it is a journalism/editorial org with no product, engineering, or vibe-coding roles — and its US-only, NYC/DC hiring posture and ongoing ownership flux make it a poor near-term fit.
